Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: Okta AI Agent webapp with ID-JAG token exchange

A production-ready web application implementing Okta AI Agent authentication
with ID-JAG (Identity Assertion JWT Authorization Grant) token exchange.

Features:
- Okta SSO authentication via ORG authorization server
- AI Agent identity with private_key_jwt (RS256)
- ID-JAG token exchange per IETF draft spec
- Token viewer showing user tokens and ID-JAG token
- Environment-based configuration (no hardcoded secrets)
- Comprehensive documentation of token flow
- Integration with NIST CSF 2.0 MCP server

Architecture:
- Users authenticate via webapp client (Web App type)
- AI Agent exchanges user ID token for ID-JAG token at ORG server
- ID-JAG token contains dual identity (user + agent)
- Agent uses ID-JAG to call MCP server on behalf of user

Technology Stack:
- Express.js with Passport OIDC
- JWT Bearer assertions (RS256)
- Session management
- Modern dark-themed UI

Documentation includes:
- Complete token dance explanation
- When to use webapp client ID vs agent ID
- ORG server vs custom server usage
- Step-by-step authentication flow
- Environment variable setup guide

Clean repository - no secrets in code or history.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire ontology clustering into API and add two-pass extraction (#31, #32)

#31: Ontology clustering
- Add POST /admin/ontology/cluster endpoint with level, threshold,
  dry_run parameters
- Wire AdminService.cluster_ontology() to build_entity/type/rel_type
  clusters with apply support
- Add apply_clusters() to write canonical labels back to DB and
  store cluster definitions in new ontology_clusters table
- Migration 008: ontology_clusters table, canonical_type on nodes,
  canonical_rel_type on edges, cluster_id FK on nodes
- Graph sidecar reload uses COALESCE(canonical_*, raw) so search
  naturally uses clustered ontology

#32: Two-pass entity extraction
- Add TwoPassExtractor: GLiNER for entities (fast, local), targeted
  LLM for relationships only (cheaper, less hallucination)
- Add relationship-only prompt (SYSTEM_PROMPT_RELATIONSHIPS) and
  extract_relationships() method on LlmExtractor
- Falls back to single-pass LLM if GLiNER sidecar unavailable
- Skips LLM pass entirely when GLiNER finds no entities (~20-30%
  of chunks)
- Config: COVALENCE_ENTITY_EXTRACTOR=two_pass activates it
- Validates LLM relationship output against GLiNER entity names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite PLAN_0_HYBRID: single Copilot SDK session in autopilot mode

Complete rewrite based on SDK API audit. The SDK (v0.1.31) has autopilot mode,
session resume, built-in workspace tools, lifecycle hooks, infinite sessions,
custom agents, and fleet/sub-agent support — none of which agentic-lib uses.

New approach: one persistent session with mission prompt replaces the
multi-session iterate loop (maintain->transform->fix x N cycles). Agent drives
its own tool loop. Hooks provide observability and budget control.

commit.message:
fix: add missing --overwrite arg + rebuild-from-scratch int8 correctness

1. Add --overwrite argparse argument (was referenced but never defined,
   causing AttributeError: 'Namespace' object has no attribute 'overwrite')

2. After fresh rebuild (no existing cache), immediately convert _pf.dat →
   _p8.dat so Dataset dequantization (* 1/METEO_SCALE) is always correct.
   Previously, rebuild loaded float16 but Dataset would divide by 12.7 →
   values shrink to [-0.79, 0.79] → wrong training signal.

3. In-memory path (no cache_dir): also quantize to int8 after transpose.

commit.message:
fix: Add input escaping and enhanced error logging for Slack notifications

Root cause analysis: Onboarding notifications were failing silently due to
unescaped special characters in user input breaking Slack API calls.

Fixes:
1. Added escapeSlackText() function to escape &, <, > characters
2. Applied escaping to all user-generated content (email, comments, channels, etc.)
3. Enhanced error logging:
   - Log payload size before sending
   - Log full error details and stack traces
   - Log failed payloads for debugging

This resolves the issue where onboarding notifications were attempted but
never completed (no success or error logs).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: kill session on Retry and escalate on CRITICAL state update failures

Two P1 fixes from #90:

1. Retry() now calls CleanupSession before clearing CurrentSession,
   preventing the old tmux session from running alongside the retry.

2. handleIdleSession fix-round and handleExitedSession now escalate
   (return error action) when store.Update fails instead of silently
   continuing with inconsistent state. The fix-round path also cleans
   up the orphaned session.

Refs #90

Fix overlay hidden behind Fragment views by using elevation

bringToFront() only adjusts z-order at call time, so new Fragment
views added later would cover the inspector overlay. Using
elevation = Float.MAX_VALUE ensures the overlay stays on top
regardless of subsequent sibling view additions.

commit.message:
Add subitizing and number bonds drills, faster difficulty progression

Two new evidence-based dyscalculia training drills:
- Subitizing: instant quantity recognition with timed dot displays
- Number Bonds: part-whole number relationships with visual bar model

Training improvements:
- Reduced difficulty cooldown from 2 sessions to 1 session
- Reduced minimum data requirement from 3 sessions to 1 session

commit.message:
Add Change Password card to Settings page for rescues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add state license management - Signup saves licenses & Settings page

## Changes

### Signup Flow (src/pages/Signup.tsx)
- Added supabase import for direct database access
- Modified handleFinalSubmit to save selected states to closer_licenses table
- Licenses are inserted with verified=false after successful account creation
- User can skip state selection (no licenses inserted)

### New Settings Page (src/pages/Settings.tsx, src/pages/Settings.css)
- Created Settings page with 4 tabs: Profile, Licenses, Billing, Notifications
- Licenses tab reuses StateSelector component
- Fetches existing licenses on mount and pre-checks them in UI
- Save computes diff: INSERT new states, DELETE removed states
- Shows success/error messages and "unsaved changes" indicator
- Glassmorphic styling matching existing auth pages

### AuthContext Updates (src/context/AuthContext.tsx)
- Modified signUp return type to include user data
- Allows Signup.tsx to access user.id for license insertion

### Navigation (src/App.tsx, src/components/Sidebar.tsx)
- Added /settings route with ProtectedRoute wrapper
- Added Settings nav item to sidebar with gear icon
- Navigation highlights active item based on current path

## Testing
- Verified with 46+ test scenarios across 8 test groups
- License add/remove immediately reflected in availability pings
- Settings page properly syncs with Supabase database
- Concurrent pings, edge cases, and bulk operations all pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release v0.1.0 — two-layer prompt injection defense MCP server

Layer 1: 7-stage deterministic sanitization pipeline (HTML, unicode,
encoded payloads, exfiltration URLs, LLM delimiters). Layer 2:
quarantined Gemini Flash-Lite Q-Agent via raw httpx REST (no SDK,
no tools, no memory). Six tools: safe_fetch, safe_read,
quarantine_fetch, quarantine_read, quarantine_scan, quarantine_stats.
SQLite blocklist for cumulative detection memory. Trust allowlist
for cost optimization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: use room.onStateChange for waiting room player count

room.state.players.onAdd/onRemove are not valid in Colyseus 0.17 —
callbacks on MapSchema require getStateCallbacks. Using onStateChange
instead, which fires on every patch including player join/leave.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add PIC symbol stubs, runtime library, and link rusty-backup on G4

- Extract emit_pic_stubs() function for malloc/free PIC trampolines
- Replace all direct `bl/b _malloc` and `b _free` with PIC stub calls
- Add .picsymbolstub1 + .lazy_symbol_pointer sections matching GCC 4.0.1
- Create rust_runtime_stubs.c: 509 symbol implementations for link
  - 89 global type instances (Vec, HashMap, File, etc.)
  - 101 self_* method stubs for filesystem operations
  - POSIX-wrapping IO functions (reader_read_exact, writer_write_all, etc.)
  - CRC32C, bitmap operations, logging, assert
  - Windows/macOS framework stubs, external crate stubs
- Create rust_reserved.s for C reserved word symbols (_if, _super)
- Result: 73/73 .o files link into 1.05 MB Mach-O PPC executable on Tiger

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address Landlock pentest findings: block OLDPWD, memfd_create, update docs

- Add OLDPWD to BLOCKED_ENV_VARS (leaked host macOS path)
- Add memfd_create to seccomp blocklist (fileless execution vector)
- Document Landlock CLAUDE.md/settings.json writability in §2 and
  why admin-owned installation fixes it

Pentest-confirmed: bypass token readability is solved by eBPF LSM
(§1), not Landlock filesystem rules. System D-Bus info leakage and
Unix socket connect() are inherent Landlock limitations.
